hidden_gems.name: Weingut Immich-Batterieberg
hidden_gems.owner: Two Hamburg families; cellar led by Gernot Kollmann
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: One of the oldest estates on the Mosel, with a cellar section first recorded in 908 AD. Since 2009 Gernot Kollmann has made dry, old-vine, ungrafted Riesling from the steep Batterieberg, Ellergrub and Steffensberg slate sites in Enkirch, a cult favourite among collectors who chase precise dry Mosel.
hidden_gems.tip: The ungrafted old-vine cuvees like Escheburg and the single-site GGs are the draw; visits are best arranged by appointment directly with the estate.
hidden_gems.description: Ancient Enkirch estate revived since 2009 by Gernot Kollmann, making dry old-vine slate Riesling that has become a cult reference for serious Mosel drinkers.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Vollenweider
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Swiss-born Daniel Vollenweider started in 2000 with barely a hectare in the Wolfer Goldgrube; the estate is now a four-star cult name working six hectares of 100 percent steep-slope Riesling. Featherlight, clear, mineral wines from a single great slate site, made in tiny quantities.
hidden_gems.tip: The Wolfer Goldgrube Spaetlese and the dry Schiefergestein are the wines to find; allocations are small, so order direct or from a specialist early.
hidden_gems.description: Tiny Traben-Trarbach estate founded in 2000, working the steep Wolfer Goldgrube slate to make precise, elegant Riesling that punches far above its few hectares.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Melsheimer
hidden_gems.address: Dorfstrasse 21, 56861 Reil an der Mosel, Germany
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A five-generation family estate in Reil, certified for ecological winegrowing since the mid 1990s and Demeter biodynamic since 2013. Thorsten Melsheimer farms steep Mullay-Hofberg slate by hand, making pure, low-intervention Riesling that bridges the classic Mosel and the natural-wine worlds.
hidden_gems.tip: Try the Vade Retro long-skin-contact bottlings alongside the classic Mullay-Hofberg Rieslings to see the estate's two faces.
hidden_gems.description: Demeter-certified Reil estate farming steep Mullay-Hofberg slate by hand, making pure biodynamic Riesling that spans classic and natural styles.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Weiser-Kuenstler
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Konstantin Weiser and Alexandra Kuenstler work just a few hectares of Trarbacher Schlossberg, Ellergrub and Gaispfad entirely by hand, organic certified for their main sites and farming biodynamically. Their slatey, clear, low-alcohol Rieslings are insider favourites that sell out fast.
hidden_gems.tip: The Trarbacher Schlossberg Kabinett is the cult bottle here; quantities are tiny, so buy from the importer the moment it lands.
hidden_gems.description: Small Traben-Trarbach estate making hand-farmed, organic-certified slate Riesling of great clarity from the Schlossberg and Ellergrub, an insider cult name.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Clemens Busch
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A VDP estate biodynamic since 2005, Clemens Busch farms the Puenderich Marienburg and bottles its grey, blue and red slate parcels separately as Falkenlay, Fahrlay and Rothenpfad. The dry, savoury, age-worthy single-parcel Rieslings are a benchmark for biodynamic Mosel.
hidden_gems.tip: Compare the grey-, blue- and red-slate GGs from the Marienburg side by side; the same grape and farming reads three different soils.
hidden_gems.description: Biodynamic VDP estate in Puenderich bottling the steep Marienburg slope by slate colour, a benchmark for dry, age-worthy organic Mosel Riesling of real depth.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Maximin Gruenhaus
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: A former Benedictine abbey estate documented at Mertesdorf since 633 and in von Schubert hands since 1882. The monopole slope splits into Abtsberg, Herrenberg and Bruderberg, giving cool, ageless Ruwer Riesling that flies under the radar next to the famous Middle Mosel names.
hidden_gems.tip: The Abtsberg Kabinett and GG are the stars; the Ruwer's cool site makes wines that age for decades yet drink beautifully young.
hidden_gems.description: Historic monopole estate in the Ruwer, in von Schubert hands since 1882, making cool, ageless Riesling from the Abtsberg, Herrenberg and Bruderberg.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Heymann-Loewenstein
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: On the dramatic terraced lower Mosel near Koblenz, this estate proved the Terrassenmosel could make world-class dry Riesling. The Uhlen and Roettgen GGs from sheer rock terraces are powerful, mineral and overlooked by visitors who never get past the Middle Mosel.
hidden_gems.tip: The Uhlen wines are split by their geological layers (Laubach, Blaufuesser Lay, Roth Lay); tasting the three together is a lesson in terroir.
hidden_gems.description: Terrassenmosel pioneer near Winningen making powerful, mineral dry Riesling from sheer rock terraces, the great and most overlooked estate of the lower Mosel.

hidden_gems.name: Weingut Stein
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Ulli Stein is a beloved Mosel maverick, farming about six and a half hectares of hard-to-work steep slate ecologically since the estate began, with no herbicides or chemical fertiliser. Dry, light, zippy Rieslings and a rare focus on the old Elbling grape make him a natural-leaning insider hero.
hidden_gems.tip: Seek out Stein's Elbling and the steep St. Aldegunder Himmelreich Rieslings; the wines are made for the table, not the score sheet.
hidden_gems.description: Ulli Stein's maverick Bullay estate farms steep slate ecologically, making dry, zippy Riesling and rare old-vine Elbling, a natural-leaning Mosel cult.
